name: Libyan Arabic

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Libyan Arabic (liby1240).

notes: Skeleton. Libyan (Sulaymitic Maghrebi) Arabic, a Semitic (Afroasiatic) variety written in the Arabic abjad. Consonant letters are mapped to IPA; qāf ⟨ق⟩ has the voiced velar reflex [ɡ] typical of the Bedouin-type dialect, jīm ⟨ج⟩ is [dʒ], and the interdentals ⟨ث ذ ظ⟩ are retained. Short vowels are not written in the abjad and, apart from optional harakāt, are left unencoded; gemination and stress are not recovered, so a high PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Libyan Arabic (liby1240)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
